title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: Jason, Michael, and Joe discussed football. Michael and Vinnie announced Z's termination due to lack of alignment and slow content. Vinnie questioned hiring difficulties. Vinnie reached out to Christina for temporary marketing help, leading to a tense exchange with Michael over Christina's capabilities. They agreed on Christina's scope for newsletter, Slack, and landing pages. Vinnie directed content to focus on new technology and automation. Michael outlined a messaging framework and took accountability for content, suggesting reposting old content.

decisions.description: Z was terminated from the organization due to lack of alignment and slow content production.
Christina's scope of work will include the newsletter, Slack updates, and landing pages.
Content strategy will focus on new technology, automation, and the company's shift.
Michael Donovan will take accountability for all content derived from the social post topic.
Vinnie will offer Christina an agency-style arrangement for her marketing services.

actionItems.task: Work with Christina directly to ensure social posts are flowing and activity is maintained.
Be accountable for everything branching off the social post topic, including brief and workflow.
Schedule time with Kevin Breen to discuss resources, data, and piping for marketing.
Finish the connection from Sanity's AI to the content lake.
Ask Christina for a price quote for three landing pages and the newsletter.

tensions: Jason Branham questioned similarity in hiring difficulties between Z and Kin.
Tense exchange between Vinnie Micciche and Michael Donovan regarding Christina's capabilities and role.
Vinnie Micciche expressed frustration over Z's content not aligning with company direction.
referencesToPast: Previous efficient workflow for social posts
Leadership call discussions on 'automotive co-pilot' and 'dealer MRI'
Successful marketing efforts from the second quarter
Lack of visibility from September to mid-October
Top-performing content from the last 60-90 days or Q1/Q2

contributions.description: Shared personal betting predictions on the Patriots for Super Bowl and AFC East.
Announced Z's termination due to lack of alignment and unsustainable content production.
Called Z a 'fraud' who said the right things but 'turned out to be a dud'.
Revealed reaching out to Christina for temporary marketing assistance.
Emphasized Christina's strengths in newsletter, questioning social post assignment.
Proposed Christina focus on newsletter, Slack posts, and landing pages.
Directed content strategy to focus on new technology and company's shift to automation.
Took accountability for all content derived from the social post topic.
Suggested reposting top-performing content from the last 60-90 days for immediate posts.
Demonstrated using ChatGPT with leadership call transcript to generate marketing messaging.
Asked if the team could duplicate effective Q2 marketing efforts for quality leads.
Affirmed Q2 success involved a content flywheel with multi-touch engagement and content diversity.
Described significant company shift from 'getting work done' to building technology to automate.
Outlined a messaging framework: problem, outcome when solved, and cost of inaction.
